prerequisites: A Fleetio account on a Professional or Premium plan (API access is only available on these plans).
An API Key generated from Settings > Manage API Keys. The key inherits the permissions of the user who created it.
Your Account Token, found below your API keys on the Manage API Keys page (it is also the alphanumeric code in your Fleetio URL after https://secure.fleetio.com/).

authMethods.name: API key (Authorization token) + Account Token
authMethods.summary: Fleetio authenticates every request with two credentials sent as HTTP headers: the API key passed as 'Authorization: Token <api_key>', and the account identifier passed as 'Account-Token: <account_token>'. Provide both in the connector's api_key and account_token fields.

setupSteps.title: Confirm plan eligibility
setupSteps.body: Ensure your Fleetio account is on a Professional or Premium plan. API access is only available to customers on those plans.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to Fleetio and open Account Menu > Settings, then click Manage API Keys in the sidebar. Click + Add API Key, enter a descriptive label, choose the API version, and save. Copy the generated key. The key is tied to your user and inherits your permissions, so use a user that can access the data you want to sync.

setupSteps.title: Find your Account Token
setupSteps.body: On the Manage API Keys page, locate the Account Token listed below your API keys. Alternatively, it is the alphanumeric code in your Fleetio URL after https://secure.fleetio.com/ (for example, abc123 in https://secure.fleetio.com/abc123/dashboard).

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated API key into the api_key field and the Account Token into the account_token field. Both are required. Keep these values secret and avoid committing them to source control.

sources.title: Fleetio Developer Portal - Quick Start (authentication headers)
Fleetio Help Center - API Keys (creating keys, plan requirements, Account Token)
